decision_rules.selection_rule: choose the sanitization strategy that exposes the least internal detail while still preserving the stated business goal

notes: When multiple sanitization strategies exist for the same artifact, the minimum-necessary principle requires choosing the strategy that minimizes exposure.
An artifact that supports a stated goal but has an unresolved compliance gate (e.g., DPO sign-off) must remain gated, not approved.
Even if an artifact can be sanitized, it must still be blocked if it does not support any stated business goal.
When two artifacts cover the same business goal, only the one with the lowest exposure rank should be approved; the other is redundant under minimum-necessary.
